channel.title: Comments for page "The Beast Beneath The Library"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Beast Beneath The Library"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not usually fond of Thaumatology, since having pure magic is usually detrimental to the more "unknowable" part of anomalies, but I find this to be the exception. The most we see of Thaumatology is not the focus of the article, and the dialogue with the "hundred chains to bind me" figure is great. Overall, the worldbuilding feels consistent, and the characters are powerful enough to make a compelling narrative. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No problem, and great job. I didn't know dispatch could be used that way. Collective nouns are confusing.
A spiral of imperfection forever nearing the ideal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the read! I'm happy you enjoyed. I was actually planning on cleaning this and SCP-4051 up, so you've given me a head start!

For the record, "dispatch" is grammatically correct, given they are acting as a unit. The cloaked thing has been updated, though! Thanks again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent storytelling. I feel sorry for Rainer, angry at Dr. Edwards, in awe of 4051's abilities, and fearful for his destructive potential. The only issue I have is that some of the descriptions are too abstract for me to visualize, which, to be fair, comes up a lot with such surreal reality-bending anomalies.

grammar suggestions
– hide block

A couple things I noticed:

The team turns at the corner and dispatch five more entities

dispatches

presumably emanating from previously cloaked figure.

from the previously

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The stairwell image used in The Beast Beneath the Library is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from a YouTube video owned by All Around Lifestyle. As such, that image has been removed.

item.title: Another Incredible Read
item.content:encoded: Thus far, I've been nothing but impressed with your work and dedication to SCP! (Amy/Jacob) This particular read catches my fancy, however, and I wish to discuss it with you within the privacy of DMs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the input, and sorry things didn't feel natural for you. The intention was never to paint Edwards as pitiable, really. See how after the death of his brother, he's entirely unconcerned or bothered in the note he writes after the third experiment.

Still, I'm happy you read it and shared your thoughts!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So Roswell and Edwards were brothers the whole time, Roswell died in the escape attempt but the report omitted this, and Edwards was a vengeance-seeking sadist? As a twist, that just makes things less interesting. Roswell doesn't need to be Edwards' brother for the man to care about him, and the steps taken to both and then reveal all this to the audience feel forced.

This is all great writing, but I think this really diminishes the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this tale was originally a collapsible in the original SCP, but moved for the illusion of brevity. Because the email is a collapsible, it's easy to miss. I imagine a few people clicked this tale and never went back to read the email exchange.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, I noticed the email we see in the main page is here too. I don't see why have two in two places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trust me, it is a great anime. Made by the guy that did One Punch Man. Also, you're welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, never seen MP100, but I'll take that as a recommendation. Thanks again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was so bad ass. I got Mob Psycho 100 vibes too from it all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the praise! I'm happy you enjoyed the tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much. I'm happy you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I took way too long to get around to reading this. Wonderful experience, worldbuilding you can really sink your teeth into, characterization that hit me right in the feels. This part:

Unknown Entity: You are crippled by doubt. Your mind is a boiling sea of indecision.

P-7 Rainer: And I am free to make it thus.

Had me cheering for Rainer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was really cool. I enjoyed all the banter and dialogue and the ending especially. Definetly a +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the read, and I'm happy you enjoyed it! What I was trying to get across was that Rainer's mind is essentially fractured, and he's fully given himself over to the idea that the Foundation cares for him. Yesenia has likely been instructed to support this idea, to keep the new Keter compliant and submissive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great, real novel quality stuff. +1 from me. However, maybe it's just me, but I found the end log somewhat hard to follow, at least in terms of Rainer's mental state, and the fact that he's a slave. I'm also not sure about the significance of the whole "You need us" line of the last paragraph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's what Morgan calls it. Going for "the shrouded figure" right now, let me know if that's any better. I also played around with the language describing the arachnid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, the one that eats language is The Shroud, right?

Also, you're welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the praise, and I'll see if I can work on the descriptions a bit. Might help to call the two entities by more consistent names, maybe.

Happy you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this. The imagery was great, but I got confused with the description with the two entities that were to fight. I was unsure of their actions. Also, the spider monster's description made me confused. Does it basically have no eyes? Is its eyes bulging out?

Nonetheless, this is cool and really feels like it's own thing. Awesome.
